---
title: "taxodist"
subtitle: "Taxonomic Distance and Phylogenetic Lineage Computation"
---

`taxodist` is an R and Python package for computing phylogenetic distances between any two taxa. Given two taxon names, `taxodist` retrieves their full hierarchical lineages from The Taxonomicon database and computes a dissimilarity index based on the depth of their most recent common ancestor (MRCA).

[![CRAN status](https://img.shields.io/cran/v/taxodist?color=green)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=taxodist) &nbsp; [![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/taxodist?color=green)](https://pypi.org/project/taxodist)

## Core Functionality

### Mathematical Properties of the Distance Metric
The distance metric implemented in `taxodist` is defined as:

$$d(A, B) = \frac{1}{\text{depth}(\text{MRCA}(A, B))}$$

Where $\text{MRCA}(A, B)$ represents the deepest shared node (the most recent common ancestor) between the lineages of taxon $A$ and taxon $B$ in The Taxonomicon hierarchy. 

Unlike simple rank-difference methods or Jaccard-based taxonomic dissimilarity indices, this metric satisfies the properties of a tree metric. Crucially, it guarantees that any two taxa sharing a given MRCA are equidistant from any third taxon outside of their shared clade, regardless of subsequent lineage branching or varying lineage depths below the split. This property is necessary for biological correctness in downstream multivariate analyses.

### Data Source and Hierarchical Depth
All taxonomic hierarchies are retrieved programmatically from **The Taxonomicon** database (Systema Naturae 2000, curated by S.J. Brands). 

Because the classification scheme incorporates extensive intermediate ranks (such as Magnorders, Parvorders, Legions, and Tribes), lineages often resolve up to 70 distinct nodes for well-documented clades. This provides significantly higher resolution than alternative programmatic databases (e.g., the Open Tree of Life or NCBI Taxonomy) which often omit intermediate hierarchical levels.

### Statistical and Ecological Workflows
The distance computation functions return objects natively compatible with standard scientific environments in both R and Python:

*   **Multivariate Ecology:** The output of `distance_matrix()` is a symmetric numeric matrix of class `"dist"` in R. It integrates directly with multivariate ecological workflows, such as those in the `vegan` package, including taxonomic distinctness analysis (`taxondive()`), Mantel test correlations (`mantel()`), and Permutational Multivariate Analysis of Variance (`adonis2()`).
*   **Phylogenetic Reconstruction:** Cluster hierarchies computed via `taxo_cluster()` (defaulting to the UPGMA algorithm) can be converted into standard `phylo` tree objects using `ape::as.phylo()` for standard tree plotting and comparative phylogenetic analysis.
*   **Ordination:** Classical Multidimensional Scaling (Principal Coordinate Analysis, PCoA) is supported natively via `taxo_ordinate()`, projecting the calculated distance space into lower dimensions.

### Network Safety and Memory Caching
To maintain performance during large community queries and prevent unnecessary load on remote servers, the package implements a local caching pipeline:

*   **In-Memory Cache:** Queried lineages are preserved in a session-level cache. Its footprint and contents can be monitored using `cache_info()`.
*   **Cache Serialization:** The current session cache can be serialized to disk as an `.rds` file using `save_cache()` and subsequently reloaded in new sessions using `load_cache()`, ensuring full reproducibility of offline pipelines.
*   **Fault Tolerance:** Network queries incorporate robust error-handling. If remote servers time out or fail to resolve, the package reports the warning gracefully through native system handlers rather than interrupting execution.

## Interactive Web Application

A Shiny web application provides access to the core package features—allowing you to search taxa, calculate pairwise distances, visualize lineages, and check coverage.

[Launch App ↗](https://3w6g1b-rodrigo-villa.shinyapps.io/taxodist_app/){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

*Supported languages: English, Português, Español, Français, Deutsch.*

## API Reference Overview

Below is a summary of the core functions available in both packages:

| Category | Function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **Distance Computation** | `taxo_distance()` | Pairwise distance between two taxa |
| | `distance_matrix()` | Full symmetric distance matrix for a set of taxa |
| | `focal_distances()` | Distances from one focal taxon to a community |
| | `closest_relative()` | Nearest taxon among a set of candidates |
| | `mrca()` | Most recent common ancestor of two taxa |
| **Lineage & Exploration**| `get_lineage()` / `get_lineage_by_id()` | Retrieve complete taxonomic lineages |
| | `taxo_search()` | Resolve taxon names and manage homonyms |
| | `compare_lineages()` / `shared_clades()` | Compare lineage hierarchies side-by-side |
| | `is_member()` / `filter_clade()` | Clade membership tests and set filtering |
| **Multivariate Analysis**| `taxo_cluster()` | Hierarchical clustering (UPGMA) |
| | `taxo_ordinate()` | Principal Coordinate Analysis (PCoA) |
| | `taxo_heatmap()` | Matrix visualization with dendrograms |
| **Cache Management**    | `save_cache()` / `load_cache()` | Serialize and restore session cache |

: Core API Functions {.striped .hover}


## Installation and basic usage

::: {.panel-tabset}

### R

```r
install.packages("taxodist")
library(taxodist)

# Pairwise distance
taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")
#> Distance : 0.01538   MRCA: Tyrannoraptora (depth 65)

taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Homo")
#> Distance : 0.02778   MRCA: Amniota (depth 36)

taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Quercus")
#> Distance : 0.25000   MRCA: discaria (depth 4)

# Distance matrix and clustering
taxa <- c("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Triceratops",
          "Homo sapiens", "Panthera leo", "Quercus robur")
mat <- distance_matrix(taxa)
cl  <- taxo_cluster(taxa)
plot(cl)
```

### Python

```python
pip install taxodist
import taxodist as td

# Pairwise distance
td.taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")
# distance: 0.01538   mrca: Tyrannoraptora (depth 65)

td.taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Homo")
# distance: 0.02778   mrca: Amniota (depth 36)

# Distance matrix and clustering
taxa = ["Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Triceratops",
        "Homo sapiens", "Panthera leo", "Quercus robur"]
mat = td.distance_matrix(taxa)
cl  = td.taxo_cluster(taxa)
cl.plot()
```

:::

## Data Source and Citation

All lineage data is sourced from [The Taxonomicon](http://taxonomicon.taxonomy.nl), based on *Systema Naturae 2000* (Brands, S.J., 1989 onwards). Please cite this resource in any published work that uses `taxodist`:

> Brands, S.J. (1989 onwards). *Systema Naturae 2000*. Amsterdam, The Netherlands. Retrieved from The Taxonomicon, <http://taxonomicon.taxonomy.nl>.
